topic: A student is accused of plagiarism
raw_output:
[story 1]

The fluorescent lights of the library hummed, a constant, irritating buzz that seemed to vibrate directly behind my eyes. Every time I glanced up from the essay, a shadow seemed to shift in the periphery of my vision. I meticulously re-read the citation guidelines, tracing the rules with my finger until the paper felt slick. Someone was watching. I could feel the weight of their gaze, heavy and judging, even though the room was empty save for the distant tapping of keyboards. My hands were clammy; I kept flexing my fingers under the table, a tiny, restless movement that I hoped no one noticed. The accusation hung in the air, a cold, sharp thing. I checked the clock again—three minutes remaining. If I just move, if I just look away, maybe the scrutiny will dissipate.

[story 2]

"Are you sure you’ve done everything right?" Professor Albright’s voice was smooth, but the inflection carried a distinct edge of suspicion. My stomach clenched, a tight, hard knot forming just beneath my ribs. I forced a smile, a brittle thing that felt alien on my face. I looked down at my notebook, the ink seeming to swim before my eyes. Every sentence felt exposed, a vulnerability waiting to be exploited. I started tapping my pen against the desk, a rapid, uneven rhythm that I tried to mask with focused breathing. The air felt too thick, too charged, as if the very molecules in the room were holding their breath, waiting for me to slip up, to reveal the hidden scaffolding of my work.

[story 3]

I kept running my thumb over the worn cover of my textbook, a repetitive, soothing motion against the rising tide of unease. The hallway outside the seminar room seemed unnaturally long, the muted sounds of other students echoing too loudly. Each footstep on the linoleum floor sounded like a deliberate intrusion. I focused intensely on the texture of the paper in front of me, willing the swirling anxiety in my chest to settle down. When the professor finally approached, his shadow fell across my desk, momentarily eclipsing the light. I could feel my skin prickling, a defensive tension coiling tight in my shoulders. I swallowed hard, my throat suddenly dry, and tried to project an air of calm competence, though inside, a frantic internal monologue was screaming warnings.
